purpose: Keep portable Rx reliance evidence useful without turning the sidecar into a second clinical or patient-identification database.

wire_rules.patient_reference: HMAC-derived pairwise reference scoped to one relying party; direct patient, member, prescription, and medical-record identifiers are forbidden.
wire_rules.source_records: Consent, diagnosis, laboratory, denial-rationale, and other source records remain in the holder's access-controlled evidence vault.
wire_rules.commitments: Low-entropy source records use domain-separated HMAC-SHA-256 commitments, not bare hashes, and every signed artifact names the privacy-key identifier needed for rotation.
wire_rules.signed_artifacts: Each artifact type has an exact field allowlist. Unknown fields are rejected before signing and before acceptance.
wire_rules.appeal_route: The portable denial artifact may carry only a generic HTTPS portal route without a query, fragment, credentials, or instance identifier.
wire_rules.errors: Closed verdicts and coded checks may cross the boundary; raw caller values and free-text clinical reasons may not.

appeal_bundle.shape: A privacy-safe projection with keyed references to the challenge, packet, receipt, authority proof, and trust roots; it never recursively embeds the submitted transaction.
appeal_bundle.retention_note: The relying party sets retention and legal-hold policy. Thirty days is an implementation default, not a legal recommendation.

disclosure_gate.decision: A bundle is disclosed only when its audience, purpose of use, privacy-policy hash, retention window, allowed artifact classes, and privacy-key scope match a relying-party-pinned profile.
disclosure_gate.exact_shape: Unknown top-level or nested bundle fields are refused before disclosure so the projection cannot become a PHI tunnel.

key_requirements.separation: Use deployment-managed sector keys distinct from receipt, authority, and artifact signing keys.
key_requirements.rotation: Every artifact and appeal projection carries a non-secret key identifier. Retain old keys only for the authorized evidence-retention period.

limitations: A malicious issuer can encode sensitive data into an otherwise permitted opaque token; issuer governance and output monitoring remain required.
Keyed commitments are independently comparable only by parties authorized to possess the sector key or the underlying source record.
A disclosure verdict is a technical minimum-necessary policy decision, not a claim of HIPAA compliance, legal authorization, or NCPDP adoption.
This profile is a data-minimization control, not a claim of legal compliance or an NCPDP-adopted standard.
